name: Acid Green
notes: Extroverted: Acid Green is one of the newest members of the Porsche special color palette and was derived from the color of the brake callipers in the 2000 hybrid models. Porsche developed the modern accent color for the E-Hybrid PHEV vehicles, visually ushering in the era of electromobility in sports car construction. The vibrant neon green can be seen for the first time as the overall exterior paint finish on the 918 Spyder super sports car. Acid Green can be described as a powerful neon green with lighter yellow undertones. The color is more saturated than a light green and is more yellowish in tone than a Greenyellow. Acid Green makes a clear statement: The bright color emphasises the high-performance character of the vehicle in a decidedly extroverted way.

name: Adria Blue
notes: Adria Blue presents as a calm, slightly muted blue that shifts subtly with changing light. Hints of gray soften the color, creating an elegant, timeless appearance well suited to classic Porsche shapes.

name: Adriatic Blue
notes: Adriatic Blue was offered on the 914, whose color palette was sourced from Volkswagen rather than Porsche. This non-metallic medium blue is roughly equivalent to Porsche’s Arrow Blue and takes its name from the Adriatic Sea, which separates the east coast of Italy from the Balkans.

name: Aetna Blue (Etna Blue)
notes: Timeless, classic and elegant: Aetnablue is a historical colour with a clear reference to the early years of Porsche. The well-known shade of blue was available on the legendary Porsche 356 B in the early 1960s and was also called 'Ätnablau' or 'Etnablue'. The colour code at that time was R740 or 6003. In 2009, Aetnablue returned to the 911 (997) under the current colour code. This shade of blue is subtle and has an impressive slight hint of grey and pastel undertones. Aetnablue gives vehicles a timeless and classic design, the color composition leaves a calming feeling of serenity and elegance. Aetnablue gained further popularity as the exterior color of the 911 Targa 4S Exclusive Design Edition (991.2) from 2016. The special edition enjoys a refined attention to detail and was only available for a limited time.

name: Aga Blue
notes: Offered briefly on short-wheelbase 911s, Aga Blue is distinguished by its subdued character and hints of gray and green beneath the blue surface. The color reads darker and more serious in shade, while sunlight reveals its complexity and depth, making it feel understated rather than bold.

name: Agate Grey Metallic
notes: Porsche simply wouldn't be Porsche without a metallic dark gray like this on the palette.

name: Alaska Blue Metallic
notes: Most 914 colors were sourced from Volkswagen rather than shared with the 911. Despite this, the palette was equally appealing. Alaska Blue Metallic is a darker metallic blue distinguished by a faint green undertone.

name: Albert Blue
notes: Historical appeal: Albertblue is a classic Porsche colour that is still highly valued by collectors today due to its blue elegance and historical connection to the Porsche brand. The dark shade of blue has been available since the 1970s, and both the colour codes and the availability on the respective models have changed over the years. In 1970, Albertblue was available under 1810 and 1818 for the 911 Targa and the Coupé. This was followed by the 911 in 1972 under the color code 325 and the 928 in 1978 under the color code 387. Albertblue is a rich, non-metallic shade of blue. The color nuance is between navy and medium blue.

name: Alex Grey
notes: Alex Grey is a color offered in the updated Paint To Sample program for 2023.

name: Algarve Blue Metallic
notes: Algarve Blue Metallic is a deep metallic blue that changes character with lighting, appearing dark and inky in shade and richer and more vibrant in sunlight. The color is named after the Portimão Circuit, located in Portugal’s Algarve region, a modern motorsport venue known for its dramatic elevation changes and coastal setting. Fine metallic flake adds depth, and in certain conditions the paint can reveal a faint green undertone, giving Algarve Blue Metallic a complex and contemporary appearance.

name: Almond Beige Metallic
notes: Almond Beige Metallic sits between champagne and light bronze — warm and luminous, but never gaudy. Its fine metallic flake gives the surface a subtle shimmer, like brushed satin catching sunlight. In direct light, the hue leans toward a soft, golden almond tone with a creamy warmth; in shadow, it deepens to a silvery-taupe with a faint bronze cast. It has that uniquely Porsche balance of sophistication and restraint — polished enough for a grand touring 928, timeless enough for a 911 SC. Almond Beige Metallic feels composed and mature — and is slightly redder than the shade of Kalahari Beige metallic that it replaced.

name: Alpine White
notes: Alpine White, a very bright white that evokes a glacier in the Swiss Alps. The color comes from the VW/Audi palette, so naturally, it's generally found on Audi-assembled transaxle cars like the 924 and the 944. It’s a clean, neutral white with just the slightest hint of warmth — not blue, not creamy — giving it a bright, precise look that feels both modern and classic. In sunlight, it gleams like fresh snow under a clear sky — brilliant but never harsh. In softer light, it smooths out to a pure, milky white that flatters the car’s shape, highlighting the precision of Porsche’s design language. There’s no trick here, no metallic flake or pearl — just honest, high-gloss clarity.

name: Amaranth Red Metallic
notes: Amaranth Red Metallic carries a quiet warmth, a soft red infused with orange metallic flake that glows under light. Its name draws from the amaranth, a plant that bursts into deep red bloom as the seasons change, mirroring the color’s mix of energy and calm restraint.

name: Amaranth Violet
notes: Amaranth is a bright non-metallic purple named after a plant known for blooms that are not coincidentally, bright purple in color.

name: Amazon Green Metallic
notes: Blue meets green: as the name of the color Amazon Green Metallic (Amazonasgruenmetallic) suggests, this historic shade reflects the magnificent natural landscape and the vibrant colour spectrum of the Amazon region. The striking, nature-inspired color was already available as "Amazonasgruen Pearlcolour" in the early 1990s on the 928, 968 and 964 model series, and has enriched the Paint to Sample range with its color depth since 2025. Amazon Green Metallic is a shimmering and memorable color that appears more blue than green and exudes a calm serenity.

name: Amethyst Metallic
notes: An elegant touch: Amethyst Metallic is inspired by the classic violet color of the semi-precious stone of the same name, amethyst, and adds a rich, luxurious look to any design. The vibrant color ranges from a light lavender to a deep violet and was available in a metallic finish for the 911 and 928 model series since the early 1990s. Amethyst Metallic is available as a Paint to Sample option for all models. The metallic sheen embodies elegance and opulence, with the shimmering effect reminiscent of a gemstone's brilliance – timeless yet subtly striking. This paint finish is also familiar from a testimonial vehicle. On a Porsche advertising poster and an advertisement from 1993, the ‘30 Years 911’ anniversary model in Amethyst Metallic is shown from the rear with the text ‘Cars have created independence for over 100 years. One has created dependence.’ The all-wheel-drive Carrera 4 variant of the 964 with a wide Turbo body is limited to 911 units and is available in three paint finishes – in addition to Amethyst Metallic, it is also available in the additional standard color Polar Silver Metallic and in Viola Metallic exclusively for the German market.

name: Amethyst Pearl
notes: Amethyst Pearl is an iridescent black cherry. It's another one of those Porsche colors that is both somewhat uncommon and very attractive, leading one to question why buyers overlooked it.

name: Ancona Blue Metallic
notes: Ancona Blue Metallic was a 914 color sourced from Volkswagen’s palette rather than shared with the 911. The shade closely resembles Porsche’s Gemini Blue and takes its name from a coastal town along Italy’s Adriatic Sea.

name: Anthracite Brown Metallic
notes: There's been a small resurgence of brown in the second decade of this century, and Anthracite Brown is part of it. A bit more grey than the similar shade of Macadamia Brown, it's an attractive color, particularly on a Boxster or a Cayman.

name: Anthracite Metallic
notes: This is a Paint To Sample (PTS) BMW color.

name: Apple Green (Daphne Green)
notes: Apple Green (Daphne Green): A bright, refreshing hue that captures the essence of a crisp, newly harvested green apple or the lush, vibrant foliage of springtime. This bold shade of green is full of energy and cheerfulness, standing out with a clean, light, and slightly yellowish cast. It evokes feelings of vitality, nature, and growth. Apple Green (Daphne Green) is dynamic and uplifting, often associated with a retro-modern aesthetic from the mid-20th century, bringing a playful and optimistic feel to any setting.

name: Apricot Beige
notes: A color that only Porsche could pull off — soft, creamy beige with a warm peach undertone that feels straight out of the late ’70s and early ’80s. It’s equal parts retro charm and quiet confidence. In sunlight, it glows with a subtle pastel warmth; in the shade, it takes on a muted, almost café-au-lait tone. It’s not trying to be flashy — it’s for the owner who knows that true style doesn’t need to shout. Apricot Beige has that vintage Stuttgart cool — the kind of color that looks just as right cruising a coastal road as it does parked next to a guards-red 911, daring you to look twice. Porsche Apricot Beige is probably one of the rarest of Porsche beiges.

name: Aqua Blue Metallic
notes: Remarkably refreshing: Aquabluemetallic is a vibrant and historic Porsche color with a metallic sheen reminiscent of the natural gemstone aquamarine. The gemstone is known for its pale blue-green tones and reflects the vastness of the ocean. Metallic blue colors have a long tradition at Porsche – among their ranks are the historic 'Aquamarinemetallic' of the 356 era or 'Aquamarinebluemetallic' from 2009. 'Aquabluemetallic' has been available since the early 2000s under the current color code for the 911 (997), 718 Boxster (986, 987) and Panamera models. Aquabluemetallic shades can range from a pale blue-green to a deeper turquoise. The metallic surface creates a subtle shimmer effect that evokes feelings of calm and relaxation. This shade of blue is a rare vehicle color that represents a bold and modern look. A dynamic color that guarantees a fresh and versatile look. As part of the '50 years of the 911' anniversary year, Porsche is honoring its 5 million fans on Facebook with a truly unique product. The 911 Carrera 4S '5M Porsche Fans' will be produced in Aquabluemetallic based on the configuration that won the most votes on Facebook and will be an extraordinary highlight project in 2013.

name: Aquamarine
notes: Aquamarine is a light, bright blue that leans toward turquoise, giving it a lively and unmistakably period-correct appearance. The color was popular on early Porsche models and reflects the playful, optimistic palette of the era.

name: Aquamarine Blue
notes: Aquamarine Blue represents another evolution of Porsche’s metallic electric blue theme. While the name and exact shade may vary by era, its roots can be traced back to classic colors such as Gemini Blue and Minerva Blue from the 1970s.

name: Aquamarine Metallic
notes: Aquamarine Metallic dates to a time when metallic colors were far less common than they are today. During the 356 era, the few metallic shades offered were notable for their elegance, and this light-to-medium metallic blue is a strong example of that restrained, timeless approach.

name: Arctic Grey
notes: Arctic Grey is a beautiful darker tone which matches perfectly with the newer models like the 718 Cayman GT4 RS. It is one of the more aggressive shades of grey in the porsche catalog.

name: Arctic Silver Metallic
notes: Porsche Arctic Silver Metallic was a classic icy cool silver. Arctic Silver replaced Polar Silver after 1996, but it's difficult to tell the two shades apart.

name: Arena Red Metallic
notes: Arena Red Metallic is a luminous medium metallic red that, in direct sunlight, glows with a ruby-like magenta depth. It’s a striking and elegant shade, handsome on any Porsche, making its relative rarity all the more surprising. Notably, it’s one of the few colors to bridge both the air-cooled and water-cooled 911 eras, appearing on the 993 and later on the 996.

name: Arrow Blue
notes: Agile appearance: Arrowblue is a vibrant shade of blue that is considered daring due to its intensity and is now highly valued by Porsche collectors. Since 1976, the color has been available under the color code 305 for the 911, 912 and 928 model series – at that time under the name Arrow Blue. The color was discontinued in 1982 and is now available as a Paint to Sample option. Arrowblue is a dynamic shade of blue that gives a refreshing look. Vehicles in Arrowblue are classified as medium to dark blue with slightly cool undertones. The color exudes sportiness and is associated with speed, agility and precision.

name: Arrow Grey Metallic
notes: Arrow Grey Metallic is a color offered in the updated Paint To Sample program for 2023.

name: Ascari Blue Metallic
notes: Time for an adventure: Ascaribluemetallic is a vibrant and eye-catching shade of blue, named after the Ascari racetrack in Spain. The powerful and dynamic color comes from Audi and is used for the high-performance R8 model. Ascaribluemetallic is a medium shade of blue and is associated with speed, power and high energy. The bold color is a popular option that appeals to people with a sense of excitement and adventure.

name: Ascot (Shamrock/Speedway) Green
notes: Ascot Green replaced Viper Green in the Porsche palette. It's a visually similar shade (it is a touch less blue and a bit more yellow), that is often referred to as "Shamrock Green." The Germans call it "Speedway Green." Sadly, it was never a common color in the U.S., although it was a bit more popular in Europe.

name: Ashleygreen
notes: New and inspiring: Ashleygreen is a medium green that was created as a result of the exclusive collaboration between the sports car manufacturer and the American designer and influencer Sean Wotherspoon. An art car consisting of the colours: Nashyblue, Seanpeach, Lorettapurple and Ashleygreen. The origin story behind the colour name Ashleygreen involves a personal connection to the designer, as Sean Wotherspoon named the newly created colour after his wife. In 2025, the modern-looking Ashleygreen will be added to the Paint to Sample product range and can be ordered by all Porsche enthusiasts.

name: Atlas Grey Metallic
notes: Porsche Atlas Grey Metallic is one of the most recent variations on the time-honored gunmetal grey theme. It's quite a bit darker than colors like Porsche Meteor Grey and Porsche Seal Grey.

name: Aubergine
notes: Subtle nuances: Aubergine is a dark, burgundy-red violet that embodies exclusivity in its appearance while also radiating the same feeling. The name goes back to the rich color of the aubergine nightshade plant and is a historic Porsche color that can be found on the 911 models of the early 1970s. The shade Aubergine has its own distinctive depth, which can range from an almost black violet to a lighter and more vibrant mix of colors. With its nuanced appearance, Aubergine creates a luxurious feeling of drama and creativity. One well-known example is the best-seller 911 Carrera RS 2.7 from 1973 – a total of 16 vehicles of this puristic model were painted in Aubergine.

name: Aubergine Metallic
notes: A very rare and beautiful purple, Aubergine Metallic looks classy on all models. It is one of the most rare purples you will ever see on a Porsche.

name: Auburn Metallic
notes: Auburn Metallic shimmers with copper warmth in sunlight, revealing deep reddish-brown undertones that shift toward rich mahogany in lower light. More refined than a true red, it balances warmth with understated distinction.

name: Auratium Green
notes: Porsche Auratium Green is a leafy medium green with some grey overtones. It's named for a lily that is native to Japan of all places. Auratium Green has recently resurfaced as a Paint-to-Sample color. Delicate blossom: Auratium Green is a true eye-catcher in many ways as well as a historical addition to the Paint to Sample program. The color was one of the first four special colours of the 356 in 1957 – a true Porsche special color. The historic vehicle color was initially only available for a few years at the end of the 1950s, and for that reason was originally only found on the 356 models. The light, candy green to luscious green hues can presumably be traced back to a type of lily that is native to Japan, and whose delicate green tone inspired the color. Auratium Green is a medium green with grey undertones.

name: Aventurine (Aventura) Green Metallic
notes: Sporty yet classy elegance: Aventurine Green Metallic is a luxurious metallic shade that conveys a harmonious impression of strength through its uniquely rich and complex qualities. This deep green is named after the gemstone aventurine, which has a pleasant green color with sparkling inclusions. Aventurine Green Metallic was initially available from 2018 for the 911 (992) as a standard colour and quickly became a popular choice for sports cars. This was followed by the adoption of the color in the series programme for the 718 (982) in 2019, the Panamera in 2021 and the Macan in 2023. Today, the color is also available through the Paint to Sample program and can be ordered by all Aventurine Green Metallic fans. The color is a muted medium metallic green with a hint of blue-grey that exudes sportiness and luxury, creating a bold yet elegant look. The shimmering effect further enhances the angular look.

name: Avocadogreenmetallic
notes: Natural appeal: Avocadogreenmetallic combines the expression of beauty and vitality with a rich, multifaceted depth. A medium to dark Green reminiscent of the colour of a ripe avocado – neither too bright nor too muted. The tone is available within the Porsche Paint to Sample programme and is also used on Audi models. The metallic effect creates an impressive colour presence with a skilfully crafted sheen, exuding a sense of refined elegance.

name: Aztec Silver
notes: Not much is known about this color, but what is known is that it is very subtle and simple. Seeing a 914 in this color would give you something to brag about to any Porsche enthusiast!

name: Azure Blue
notes: Azure Blue reflects the more conservative color choices of early Porsche 356 models, standing apart from the brighter pastels common in the 1950s. Particularly on Pre-A cars, the color feels tasteful and restrained, echoing the austerity of post-war West Germany. Azure Blue is handsome, subtle, and decidedly low-key.

name: Azure Blue 356
notes: Deep-rooted friendship: Azureblue356 is the reinterpretation of the color 'Azureblue' that Porsche developed for the 356 America Roadster in the early 1950s. The color Azureblue356 has been reissued in the Paint to Sample Plus process especially for the 911 Carrera GTS Cabriolet America Edition in 2022 to celebrate two occasions at once: the 70th anniversary of the Porsche 356 America Roadster and the 30th anniversary of the 964 America Roadster. The exterior color is a modern interpretation of the historic Azureblue, created in collaboration with experts from Style Porsche, Porsche Exclusive Manufaktur, representatives from Porsche Cars North America, Porsche Canada Limited and inspiration from the historical archive. Azureblue356 is a rich medium blue with subtle greenish undertones and is reminiscent of a deep blue sky on a clear day. The invigorating color radiates serenity and conveys a feeling of relaxation and openness.

name: Azure Metallic
notes: Azure Metallic is a light metallic silver-blue that sits between blue and silver in appearance. The color is similar to the Ferrari-inspired Azzurro California used on 50th Anniversary 911 Club Coupes, giving it a refined, modern character.

name: Azurite Blue
notes: Azurite is a soft crystalline mineral known for its deep azure blue color. In Porsche form, Azurite Blue is a non-metallic dark blue that is both rare and handsome, offering a rich, understated appearance.

name: Azurro California
notes: Azzurro California takes its name from the Italian word for blue, highlighting its Ferrari-inspired origins. The color was famously chosen for the 50th Anniversary 911 Club Coupe, limited to just 50 cars.

name: Azzuro Thetys Metallic
notes: Azzurro Thetys Metallic was offered recently and briefly through Porsche’s Paint to Sample program. The name is derived from the Greek goddess Thetis, often depicted in mythology as a sea nymph. The shade is similar to earlier colors such as Light Blue Metallic and Horizon Blue Metallic.

name: Bahama Blue
notes: Porsche Bahama Blue was a short-lived color often confused with the similar shade Arrow Blue, which replaced it in 1976. Despite the similarity, Bahama Blue has its own distinct character as a non-metallic medium blue.

name: Bahama Blue Metallic
notes: Early Audi-engine 924s are rarely seen today in any color or condition. Bahama Blue Metallic is an especially rare shade from the first two years of 924 production. Like most 924 colors, it was sourced from Audi’s palette rather than Porsche’s, and was also found on period Audi 5000 models. Despite the similar name, it is entirely unrelated to Porsche’s non-metallic Bahama Blue.

name: Bahama Yellow
notes: Porsche Bahama Yellow is a lovely warm yellow with strong butterscotch overtones. It's a color that quickly fell out of fashion after the 1970s, and a large number of Bahama Yellow Porsches were re-painted in other colors (usually red or silver). It's popular once again and many 1980s color-changed 911s and 912s have since been returned to their original Porsche Bahama Yellow.

name: Bahia Red
notes: Bahia Red was an unusual color. Not necessarily in terms of scarcity, but by the fact that it was shared across the 914 and 911 lines. Most 914 colors were of VW origin and not shared with the 911. Bahia Red is named after the Brazilian state of the same name. It's the host to the wildest and most colorful Carnival celebration in Brazil.

name: Bali Blue
notes: Historical connection: the color Baliblue has a long tradition at Porsche. It was a popular color for the 356 models in the 1960s and was later offered as one of four special paint finishes when the 911 was launched. This means that the color joins the colorful memories of the classic Porsche cars of yesteryear. Baliblue can be described as dark blue with a slight hint of eggplant purple. While the color appears elegant and subtly dark in the shade, it reveals an intense and deep luminosity in sunlight that catches everyone's eye.

name: Baltic Blue Metallic
notes: Porsche Baltic Blue Metallic, like the earlier Petrol Blue, gained modest traction among Porsche buyers. While far from common, it is not unusual to see the color on models such as the 928, 911, and 944. The shade appears to have been ordered fairly consistently across the Porsche lineup.

name: Bamboo Beige
notes: A soft, golden-beige tone with just a hint of green warmth — Bamboo Beige embodies that mid-’70s Porsche subtlety: refined, sun-baked, and effortlessly tasteful. It sits between sand and champagne, offering a natural, almost linen-like warmth without drifting into yellow or gray. In sunlight, it comes alive with a pale honey glow; under softer light, it flattens into a muted, sandy beige with a faint olive undertone.

name: Bamboo Metallic
notes: A rare, effortlessly elegant hue that sits between gold and olive — subtle, but with real character. Bamboo Metallic has that unmistakable early-’80s Porsche sophistication: warm, slightly earthy, and deeply reflective. Under sunlight, it glows like brushed champagne; under overcast skies, it takes on a muted greenish tone that feels organic and grounded.

name: Barnato Green
notes: Barnato Green is a color offered in the updated Paint To Sample program for 2023. It's named for Woolf Barnato, one of the famous racing "Bentley Boys" of the 1920s. Unique speed: The story behind Barnatogreen is just as exciting as the effects of the color itself. The deep green paint is named after Diana Barnato Walker, a British pilot who was the first woman to fly faster than the speed of sound and is the daughter of Bentley Chair Woolf Barnato. Her father also had some impressive achievements: In addition to his work as a businessman, Woolf Barnato was a member of the Bentley Boys. He took part in the 24 Hours of Le Mans three times and won just as many times – something no other racing driver has managed to do to date. Barnato Green is a rich, deep green with light blue undertones. As a sophisticated color, Barnato Green is often associated with luxury and performance, and it also conveys a feeling of power and elegance.

name: Basalt Black
notes: A beautiful shiny black that looks very elegant, or should I say Basalt Black. This black looks so clean and fresh and always seems to shine wherever it goes.

name: Basalt Black Metallic
notes: Basalt Black Metallic is as the name implies, a metallic black. In certain lights, it has bluish/purple undertones. It's named for a type of volcanic rock.

name: Beige Grey
notes: If you've read through many of these summaries, you'll know that the one consistent theme is the observation that Porsche color names fall into two categories--fanciful and literally descriptive. This rare color, Porsche Beige Grey, falls squarely into the latter category. It’s a balanced mix of warm beige and cool grey, creating a neutral tone that feels both industrial and elegant. Think sun-faded concrete with a touch of sand — calm, grounded, and timeless. In bright light, it leans toward a pale, creamy taupe with a whisper of warmth; in shade, the grey undertone comes forward, giving it a slightly steely, purposeful look. There’s no gloss or flash here — just that clean, honest hue that lets the car’s form do the talking.

name: Berber Yellow
notes: Berber was another 914-only color, a medium yellow with a touch of green. Like most 914 colors, it came from the VW palette rather than from Porsche's color list.

name: Beryll-Metallic
notes: A light blueish green that was not around long. It's a very unique color and almost makes you think about water or an ocean.

name: Biarritz White
notes: Biarritz White is a warm, creamy white with a subtle touch of ivory and the faintest champagne undertone. It’s less clinical than Glacier or Alpine White, trading sharpness for sophistication. The hue feels sun-kissed — like white sand under afternoon light — giving the car a gentle warmth that feels both classic and refined. In bright light, Biarritz White glows with a smooth, almost porcelain sheen; in shade, its creamy depth comes forward, revealing a quiet richness that pairs beautifully with tan or brown interiors. It’s the kind of color that flatters chrome, hides dust, and never feels out of place — from air-cooled 911s to front-engined GTs.

name: Birch Green
notes: Birch Green is a classic fluorescent color that suites the extroverted character of a GT car. It's similar to Acid Green.

name: Biscay Blue Metallic
notes: Porsche Biscay Blue Metallic is a light blue metallic with a subtle green undertone. Named after the Bay of Biscay off France’s Atlantic coast, the color has a soft, coastal character that shifts gently with changing light, appearing brighter and more reflective in the sun.

name: Bitter Chocolate (Cockney Brown)
notes: Bitter Chocolate is brighter and considerably redder than the color Sepia Brown which it replaced. If you can get over brown car phobia, it's really quite attractive, especially on a Carrera with gold side scripts and wheels.

name: Black
notes: In spite of the fact that it's famously difficult to keep looking good, black has been a popular Porsche color from the 1980s to the present. As an aside, it's always a bit amusing to look at ads for Porsches for sale that describe the color as "Schwarz Black." "Schwarz" is simply German for black, so it translates literally to "Black Black." Oh well, may the Schwarz be with you.

name: Black Metallic
notes: An always popular variation on basic black, the metallic version is just as hard to keep clean.

name: Black Olive
notes: Unobtrusively natural: Black Olive is a dark, muted shade of green that is reminiscent of the color of ripe black olives. The non-metallic dark green color is versatile and enjoys an intriguing look. The color was first available as a Paint to Sample option for the Porsche 911 in 2006 and then again in 2018. Black Olive is a neutral color with brownish or greyish undertones. The matte finish of the dark green surrounds the sports car with a warm and earthy mood. Black Olive is inviting and its simplicity exudes a subtle, timeless elegance. The color is associated with natural and organic materials due to its appeal.

name: Black Pearl
notes: Black pearl shines and stands out wherever it goes, which is not so usual since black is one of the most common colors. The pearl in the paint really changes everything and shows off the body lines of every model.

name: Black Pearl Effect
notes: Porsche's Black Pearl effect paint is a deep, dark black that, instead of being flat, has a shimmering quality from pearl or metallic pigments. This gives the paint a multi-dimensional effect, with its color appearing to shift subtly in different lighting conditions, enhancing the car's body lines and creating a "shimmer" or "diamond-like" sparkle. "Kinetic art": The intricate layering of primer, metallic undercoats, and special clearcoats is designed to manipulate light refraction, resulting in a finish that transforms the car into a piece of "kinetic art".

name: Blood Orange
notes: Blood Orange is a vibrant and deep orange-red Porsche paint color. It’s a relatively new color that was introduced in 2023 to honor decorated Polish rally driver Sobieslaw Zasada with a special edition 992.1 Dakar, codenamed RED58. Sobieslaw Zasada is widely regarded as one of the greatest Polish rally drivers ever. A short history lesson: Zasada was one of the drivers who participated in the grueling London to Sydney Marathon in 1968. During the over 10,000-mile race, the Polish rally legend won the harsh Australian section with an armored (against kangaroos) air-cooled 911S. The 911 Dakar RED58 special was built through Porsche’s Sonderwunsch customization program as a one-off salute to the legendary 911. It sports a unique Blood Orange body color, hand-painted "Poland" lettering on its rear cover, and the number "58" on its doors. Blood Orange is a homage to Tangerine, as Zasada’s winning 1968 911 rally car was painted in that color. But it isn’t exactly Tangerine. The two colors are almost indistinguishable side by side, but they are slightly different, owing largely to changes in how modern paints are formulated. Regardless, both the modern Blood Orange and classic Tangerine share the same German name: blutorange. There are presently only two cars in the world painted in Blood Orange: Zasada’s 992.1 Dakar in Poland and one 982 Spyder RS in North America.

name: Blue Metallic
notes: Blue Metallic is about as literally descriptive as a color name can be. The shade itself is a medium blue with a fine metallic finish that adds depth and reflectivity without pushing the color too bright. It has been used on a Porsche 914 that served as an example displayed at the Porsche Museum in Zuffenhausen.

name: Blue Turquoise
notes: Blue Turquoise is a vivid Porsche blue that sits very close to Riviera Blue and Mexico Blue, making it difficult to distinguish unless the cars are side-by-side. Compared directly, Blue Turquoise reads slightly darker and carries a greener undertone than both Riviera and Mexico.

name: Brewster Green
notes: Family tradition: both Ferry Porsche's company cars and most of the 911 vehicles owned by his descendants have one thing in common – they are often painted in subtle and elegant shades of green. Dr. Wolfgang Porsche continues this model tradition to this day, for example with Brewster Green, British Racing Green and Irish Green. Brewster Green was developed at the customer's request as part of the Paint to Sample Plus process and has become one of the most popular Paint to Sample options in recent years alongside Oak Green. Brewster Green reflects a deep green tone reminiscent of forests and untouched natural landscapes. The sophisticated and timeless shade has a complex and nuanced appearance with a deep green color base that has a slight blue-grey undertone. In certain environments, Brewster Green appears almost black and, in the right light, the green tone comes out. In keeping with the green color tradition of the Porsche family and in honor of the first Porsche Club founding members, the 911 Club Coupe was launched to mark the 60th anniversary of Porsche Clubs worldwide. The special model from Exclusive Manufaktur (type 991) in Brewster Green was limited to 13 units and could only be purchased by Porsche Club members.

name: Brilliant Black
notes: Brilliant Black may not be so brilliant but it is pretty. It didn't last long before taken out of the Porsche lineup, but nonetheless it is a simple and nice relaxing black to look at.

name: British Racing Green
notes: Captivating look: British Racing Green is a traditional shade of deep green derived from the green color of motorsport in the United Kingdom. Racing cars in British Racing Green were able to conquer top positions in the first car races at the beginning of the 20th century. The origin of the color name British Racing Green goes back to the Gordon Bennett Cup. Since Great Britain could not host the next race after its first victory in 1902, the venue was moved to Ireland. As a tribute to the host country, the Olive Green racing car was changed to a 'Clover Green'. The color had already been made available at Porsche in the late 1980s and can currently be ordered through the Paint to Sample program. British Racing Green is a versatile color that has subtle blue-grey undertones and subtle black nuances. It is considered a classic vehicle color with a timeless quality that makes a tasteful and understated impression.

name: Britishracinggreenmetallic
notes: Pure elegance: the rich tone Britishracinggreenmetallic is a deep Green shade with a metallic sheen that is immediately associated with the Green colour of British motorsport. Britishracinggreenmetallic is a modern, metallic interpretation of the classic Britishracinggreen colour, featuring a shimmering finish, giving it an extra touch of luxury and depth. The tone was originally developed by Audi and now enriches the Porsche Paint to Sample programme. The metallic finish gives the sports car a dazzling effect and ensures a timeless understatement. Britishracinggreenmetallic brings the car’s character to life with a classic, timeless aesthetic.

name: Brocade Red
notes: Porsche Brocade Red is a dark non-metallic red that was part of the early 924 palette, although it was sometimes specified on 911s and 930s via Porsche's Paint-to-Sample program. The word brocade refers to a rich, decorative silk fabric that often came in dark jewel tones.

name: Brown Metallic
notes: Porsche metallic brown colors vary, but they are generally sophisticated, warm, and elegant shades with metallic flake that adds a shimmer. Examples include the deep, earthy Argusbrownmetallic, the warm, reddish Chestnutbrownmetallic, and the rich, dark Belugabrownmetallic. Other options range from the caramel-like Nougat Brown Metallic to the lighter, creamy Truffle Brown Metallic.

name: Burgundy Red
notes: Burgundy Red is another example of Porsche picking a name that is literally descriptive of the color. Looking at a Porsche in this color makes us want to curl up with a nice glass of red wine.

name: Burgundy Red Metallic
notes: Porsche color names are either totally abstract, or literally descriptive. This one falls squarely into the latter category.

name: Bush (Leaf) Green
notes: Leaf Green is an extremely scarce color, not as vibrant as Irish Green nor as bold as Conda or Viper Green, it was overlooked by most of the Porsche-buying public. It was a more popular shade among Jaguar and Mercedes-Benz owners where it was known as Willow Green and Reed Green respectively.

name: Byzantine Gold
notes: Paint to sample Mercedes Benz color code 442H.

name: Caramel Brown
notes: At the risk of sounding like a skipping CD, Porsche color names seem to come in two categories-- hopelessly esoteric or literally descriptive. Three guesses which category Caramel Brown falls into?

name: Carbon Black Metallic
notes: Carbon Black Metallic is a very shiny black that allows the lights to reflect off from all directions and angles. It almost looks lighter than black at times especially under direct lighting.

name: Carbon Grey Metallic
notes: When you hear carbon, you always expect nice... and carbon grey metallic does not disappoint. So sleek, yet so aggressive and fast without even moving. This color looks makes the cars looks like a spaceship, but also a classy persons at the same time.

name: Carbon Steel Grey Metallic
notes: Carbon Steel Grey Metallic is a color offered in the updated Paint To Sample program for 2023.

name: Carmine Red
notes: Carmine Red is a darker, purer red than most Porsche reds which have a touch of orange in them. It takes particularly well to the GTS treatment of blacked out trim and wheels. Carmine Red is not PTS, but it is a premium paint color to purchase for your Porsche,

name: Carmona Red Metallic
notes: Porsche Carmona Red Metallic is a deep wine red metallic that looks as handsome on a Cayenne as it does on a 911. Porsche named the color for a town in Andalusia, Spain, that is known appropriately for wine-making.

name: Carrara White
notes: Carrara White is Porsche’s definitive pure white with just a whisper of warmth. It takes its name from the Italian marble, and it shares that same clean, sculptural quality — a white that flatters every curve without ever feeling cold or sterile. In full sun, Carrara White glows with a smooth, almost creamy brilliance — bright but never harsh. In softer or shaded light, it reveals a faint hint of warmth that keeps it from tipping into blue or gray territory. The result is a crisp, high-gloss white that feels luxurious yet technical — refined enough for a 911 Carrera, understated enough for a Cayman or Macan. Please do not call it Carrera White.

name: Carrara White Metallic
notes: Carrara White Metallic is Porsche’s modern benchmark for sophistication in white. Named after the famed Italian marble, it’s a pure white base enriched with a fine metallic flake that gives it a soft, almost liquid glow. The undertone is neutral — neither too warm nor too cool — striking that perfect middle ground between purity and depth. In bright sunlight, Carrara White Metallic gleams with a subtle silvery radiance, the metallic content adding depth without sparkle or glitter. In overcast light or shade, it smooths into a creamy, sculptural white that accentuates Porsche’s curves and contours with quiet precision.

name: Cartagena Yellow
notes: Porsche Cartagena Yellow was one of the launch colors for the 992.2 Carrera S. It's a striking shade of pastel yellow with a hint of green and some metallic pop. In some lights, it's similar to the unicorn 60s 911 color Lido Gold.

name: Casablanca Beige Metallic
notes: Casablanca Beige is more of a mellow gold than a beige. It's another handsome metallic color that simply couldn't compete with the early 80s popularity of colors like Guards Red and Grand Prix White. This color is enjoying a resurgence and is appreciated for it's period correct sensitivity.

name: Cashmere Beige
notes: Cashmere Beige is a warm, light beige with a gentle golden tint and just a touch of softness that feels almost tactile. It’s not a flat neutral — it has depth and warmth, like fine leather or sunlit parchment. Under strong light, it brightens to a pale champagne-beige with a smooth satin glow; in shadow, it turns richer, developing a subtle caramel tone that gives the car real presence without ever feeling showy. The hue carries that unmistakable late-’80s and early-’90s Porsche polish — mature, sophisticated, and quietly luxurious. Cashmere Beige looks at its best paired with contrasting darker trim or interiors — think black, mahogany, or saddle — bringing out its warmth and balance.

name: Cashmere Beige Metallic
notes: Cashmere Beige Metallic is a very clean and basic color to keep you under the radar while still standing out. It is a very fitting color for such a powerful beast like the Cayenne.

name: Cassis Red Metallic
notes: Porsche Cassis Red Metallic is one of the most polarizing and difficult to describe colors that Porsche has ever offered. It's kind of a purplish/pinkish light metallic bronze that varies considerably depending on the lighting. While not exactly common, the order rate was far from nil. Give it a chance, it's especially pretty in a Porsche 928 or a 911 cab, particularly when paired with a burgundy top and interior. The modern Porsche Color Frozen Berry Metallic is a bit of an homage to Cassis Red.

name: Centenaire Silver
notes: Originally launched as "Le Mans Silver Metallic" for the 2023 911 Carrera GTS Le Mans Centenaire Edition, Centenaire Silver is a luminous heritage shade marking Porsche’s 75th anniversary and Le Mans’ centenary, echoing the 1951 356 SL’s silver finish and celebrating the brand’s first triumph at the world’s most storied endurance race.

name: Ceramic (Caramel) Beige
notes: A warm, mid-tone beige with a distinctly golden undertone and just a touch of amber — imagine the color of sunlit leather or café crema foam with a hint of honey. Ceramic Beige carries more richness than a neutral tan, but stops short of full caramel; it walks that fine line between elegance and warmth. In direct light, the hue glows softly — a creamy beige with a silky yellow-gold tint that flatters the curves and edges of the car’s bodywork. In shade, it deepens into a mellow toffee tone, picking up subtle orange-brown warmth without ever turning brassy. The effect is organic and luxurious — a color that feels tactile, almost like polished hide rather than metal.. Like most similar shades, it' s rather polarizing among Porsche people.

name: Chalk
notes: Chalk is a light grey throwback to 1950s and early 1960s colors like Pearl Grey and Heron Grey.

name: Champagne Yellow
notes: Champagne Yellow is a classic early/mid-1960s color that everyone seemed to be using. Order it on a Jaguar, Triumph or an MG and it was called Primrose Yellow. Corvette and Chevelle people knew it as Butternut Yellow, and on a Mustang, it was Springtime Yellow. The precise formula might not have been the same, but dang, all of those colors look pretty much alike.

name: Cherry Black Metallic
notes: Porsche's Cherry Black Metallic is a dark, multi-faceted color that often appears black but reveals deep, dark red and burgundy undertones in sunlight. It's a Paint-to-Sample (PTS) color known for its ability to shift appearance depending on the light, giving it a mysterious and sophisticated look that can be both sporty and classy. While it can look like a dark, chocolatey burgundy or mahogany in some light, the "cherry" aspect is a rich, deep red that becomes more visible when the light hits it.

name: Cherry Metallic
notes: A beautiful dark red that shines everywhere, Cherry Metallic is a special one to add to the Porsche catalog. It fits beautifully with body lines and shows off everything exquisitely.

name: Cherry Red
notes: Cherry Red is a darker one, and it could almost be mistaken for a maroonish color in the right lighting. Beginning in 1988, it's no wonder it is now a paint to sample that is not overlooked.

name: Chestnut Brown Metallic
notes: Porsche's Chestnut Brown Metallic is a warm, rich, and sophisticated brown with reddish undertones, creating a deep and inviting appearance. It's a medium to dark shade that is both elegant and cozy, allowing the car's body lines to be visible without being overly flashy. A rich brown that looks warm and full, with hints of red undertones, similar to a chestnut nut. Feel: It evokes a sense of warmth, coziness, and classic elegance. The metallic flake gives it a sophisticated shimmer, and the exact look can shift slightly between being more brown or more grey depending on the lighting. It is a Paint-to-Sample (PTS) color, making it rarer than the standard offerings. It was introduced with the limited-edition Panamera Exclusive Series in 2014.

name: Chiffon White
notes: Chiffon White is not your typical bright white. It’s a warm, creamy off-white with a subtle beige and peach undertone that gives it a sophisticated, almost nostalgic glow. Think ivory silk or porcelain with a touch of warmth — smooth, refined, and quietly luxurious. In sunlight, it reveals a gentle, creamy radiance with hints of pale champagne; under shade, it deepens into a soft, muted beige-white that feels organic and timeless. There’s a richness to it — the kind of tone that flatters chrome trim and classic bodywork, adding depth without shouting for attention. Chiffon White captures the best of Porsche’s early-’80s refinement — elegant, cultured, and confident without flash. It’s the white of a grand tourer, not a track car — the color of restraint, craftsmanship, and enduring style.

name: China Grey
notes: China Grey is a color offered in the updated Paint To Sample program for 2023.

name: Chocolate Brown
notes: Porsche Chocolate Brown in most lighting looks almost identical to Mocha Brown.

name: Chrome Yellow
notes: For reasons likely lost to time, Saturn Yellow became Chrome Yellow for 1974. It's essentially the same color.

name: Classic Silver Metallic
notes: The name says it all, Classic Silver Metallic. It looks perfect on anything, and since day one Porsche's have always been stunning in Silver.

name: Club Blau
notes: Exclusive excitement: Porsche celebrated the 60th anniversary of Porsche Club of America (PCA) in 2015 with the striking color Clubblau. For this occasion, the Stuttgart sports car manufacturer launched the 911 GTS Club Coupe – exclusively for customers in North America and exclusively in the color Clubblau. This energetic shade of blue is derived from the PCA logo and is a reinterpretation of the logo’s original color, 'Acidblue.' It was developed as part of the Paint to Sample Plus process and is available as a Paint to Sample option. Clubblau is a paint color that is characteristic of Porsche. A bold and vibrant color, Clubblau conveys a feeling of energy mixed with excitement. The bright, vibrant look of this shade of blue is perceived as 'electric' or 'neon', meaning that Clubblau creates a sense of sportiness and displays its own expression of athleticism. The color symbolizes unique personalities and a modern flair. Only 60 examples of the special model were produced for the American market, and all 911 Club Coupes remain sought-after collector's items to this day.

name: Cobalt Blue Metallic
notes: Cobalt Blue Metallic is an intense version of a light, metallic blue tone that develops an electrifying luminosity through its shiny shimmer. The color was introduced in the later half of the 964 production run. It was often classified as a Special Order Color (Code 37U), meaning it carried a premium price when new and was less frequently selected than the "Standard" palette. Cobalt Blue Metallic is generally considered a rare and highly desirable color. While exact total production numbers by color are often difficult to pinpoint, enthusiast data and registry records confirm its scarcity compared to standard colors like Guards Red or Grand Prix White. The subtle violet undertones make Cobalt Blue Metallic appear stylish yet impressively vibrant. The shade is most likely named after the element cobalt, which is used to make the dye that creates this distinctive shade of blue. Cobalt Blue Metallic is frequently compared to Maritime Blue, another rare Porsche color, though Maritime is a non-metallic "solid" paint while Cobalt is a high-depth metallic. Enthusiasts value Cobalt Blue for its "color shift" properties. It features subtle violet undertones that cause the paint to appear vibrant and electrifying in direct sunlight while looking deeper and more muted in the shade.

name: Coffee (Cocoa) Brown
notes: Porsche Coffee or Cocoa Brown falls squarely into the literally descriptive category of Porsche color names. It replaced the similar shade of Togo Brown.

name: Coffee Beige Metallic
notes: Coffee Beige Metallic is a new color for the Taycan and represents the first pure beige shade that Porsche has offered in several decades.

name: Cognac Brown Metallic
notes: Brown can be a tough color for dealers to move, but Porsche has almost always had one to offer customers. This one was short-lived but it was a particularly rich shade of metallic caramel, evocative of the spirit for which it is named.

name: Cognac Metallic
notes: Cognac is almost identical to the 1980s color Nougat Metallic. It's handsome and understated, and looks particularly sharp on a cabriolet when paired with a brown top.

name: Colibri Green Metallic
notes: Colibri Green Metallic is a mesmerizing and highly distinctive green, named after the Spanish word for hummingbird (colibrí). This color perfectly captures the iridescent, shimmering flash of a hummingbird's plumage. It is a medium-toned green that walks the line between blue and green, featuring a significant and dynamic metallic effect. The metallic flakes are key, allowing the color to shift dramatically depending on the angle and light source—sometimes appearing a lush, cool turquoise, and other times revealing a brighter, almost emerald green depth. It is an exotic, lively, and captivating color that commands attention and conveys speed and luxury.

name: Conda Green
notes: Conda Green is a legendary and highly sought-after non-metallic color from Porsche's early 1970s palette. This assertive, period-correct bright green is deeply saturated and bold, recalling the adventurous spirit of the long-hood 911 era. It presents as a vivid, cheerful yellow-green, brighter and more yellow than Jade Green, yet often described as slightly more mellow and less "low-key" than the later, more acidic Viper Green. Conda Green is a clear, unadulterated color that embodies the exuberant, confident style of the decade it defined, making a powerful statement that is highly prized by collectors today.

name: Condor Yellow
notes: Porsche Condor Yellow is almost indistinguishable from the later shade of Porsche Champagne Yellow. Pastel yellow was in general, a popular early 1960s sports car color on both sides of the Atlantic. Jaguar and MG used a similar shade called Primrose Yellow and Corvettes and Camaros were frequently seen in Butternut Yellow. The Mustang equivalent was Springtime Yellow. As the 1960s progressed, bolder yellows became more popular, and pastels like this went out of vogue, not returning until the 1987 color Porsche Summer Yellow.

name: Connaught Green
notes: A very rare and beautiful dark green, Connaught Green is one that you will probably never see but it truly is pretty.

name: Continental Orange
notes: Continental Orange is one of those bright 70s shades that managed to stick around up to the beginning of the 911SC and 928 era. It's exceedingly rare to find one of those cars in this shade, and when they sell, the color adds a hefty premium. It's a redder orange than Signal Orange or even Tangerine and in the shade, it can be mistaken for the much more common Guards Red, but in bright sunlight.....

name: Copenhagen Blue
notes: Copenhagen Blue is a non-metallic dark blue with gray-green undertones. Overshadowed by brighter 1980s favorites like Guards Red, it remains one of the least common colors found on the Porsche 944 and is named after the capital of Denmark.

name: Coppa Florio Blue
notes: Coppa Florio Blue, sometimes called Ice or Polar Blue, is frequently mistaken for Gulf Blue. The color is actually lighter and more closely resembles Porsche Glacier Blue. Its name references the Coppa Florio, a lesser-known Italian road race associated with the Targa Florio.

name: Copper Bronze Metallic
notes: Copper Bronze is from the literally descriptive family of Porsche colors. Even brown haters can find this tasteful shade appealing.

name: Copper Brown Metallic
notes: The name tells you everything you need to know. It's often confused with Rosewood, another 80s metallic brown.

name: Copper Metallic
notes: Another literally descriptive color from Porsche. It's one of several shades that migrated from the 914 to the 924.

name: Copper Ruby Metallic
notes: Not a common color, copper ruby metallic stands warm and bright with its dark red accents.

name: Coral Metallic
notes: Porsche Coral Metallic is flat-out one of the rarest 3.2 Carrera colors. Even the most seasoned 911-spotters have never seen it or even heard of it. It's reminiscent of the 2.7 911 color Salmon Metallic, and while the latter has seen some lift via the Paint-to-Sample program, if there's a Coral PTS car out there, we haven't seen it.

name: Coral Red Metallic
notes: Porsche Coral Red is a bright metallic red with an orange cast to it. It's another in a long list of attractive Porsche colors that were inexplicably ignored by North American buyers.

name: Creme White
notes: Creme White is a soft off-white with a gentle yellow-beige undertone that gives it a rich, almost edible warmth. It’s less clinical than a pure white and less nostalgic than ivory — sitting perfectly in that sweet spot of refined subtlety and quiet sophistication. In bright light, it glows with a creamy, sunlit tone — soft and inviting rather than stark. In the shade, it settles into a mellow, buttery white with a faint parchment hue that brings out the car’s curves with understated depth. There’s nothing artificial about it — it feels organic, natural, timeless.

name: Crystal Blue
notes: Crystal Blue is often confused with Porsche Gulf Blue, though it is noticeably brighter and carries a stronger green influence. The color has a vivid, almost tropical quality that gives it a lighter, more energetic presence, especially in direct sunlight, while still feeling distinctly period-correct.

name: Crystal Green Metallic (Modern)
notes: Crystal Green is like most modern Porsche greens, attractive but seldom ordered by customers.

name: Crystal Green Metallic (Vintage)
notes: Crystal Green Metallic is a distinct, rare 1980s-era Porsche metallic finish, frequently appearing on models like the 944. It is characterized as a subtle yet luminous metallic green, often featuring a distinct, light-reflecting, and slightly teal-green or yellowish-green tone depending on lighting, offering a vintage, sophisticated look.

name: Crystal Silver
notes: Porsche Crystal Silver Metallic is a pure silver that is reminiscent of the silvery white of pure aluminum. It was a popular, and attractive Cayenne color.

name: Crystal Silver Metallic
notes: Crystal Silver Metallic is very calm and collected without catching the eye of everything around it. That doesn't mean its beauty goes unnoticed.

name: Dalmatian Blue (Oxford Blue)
notes: Dalmatian Blue, also known as Oxford Blue or Sea Blue in German, is one of the darker Porsche shades of medium blue. The color is particularly well suited to the lines of the 911, offering a rich, composed appearance. Its name is derived from the Dalmatian Coast of Croatia.

name: Dark Blue
notes: Porsche Dark Blue (dunkelblau) is a classic non-metallic navy blue with a deep, understated character. In certain light it can appear nearly black, while brighter conditions reveal a rich blue tone that subtly highlights the car’s lines.

name: Dark Blue Metallic
notes: Authority on wheels: Darkbluemetallic is a strong color that embodies calm and stands for harmony. Dark blues, in particular, symbolize the dream of freedom through their close association with natural phenomena such as the depth of the sea or the view of the sky. Darkbluemetallic represents prudence and objectivity while conveying a feeling of security. This is also the reason why certain industries and companies like to have their logos in a shade of blue. Darkbluemetallic is a deep, shiny shade of blue that can also be described as indigo-, navy- or midnight blue. The paint is versatile and the metallic effect makes it appear very dark, but not quite as dark as black.

name: Dark Olive Metallic
notes: The right mood to suit: Dark Olive Metallic is a sophisticated color that can be used to create a variety of moods and styles in one design. The dark green shade reflects the color of black olives, whose intensity generally ranges from dark green to black. With its pleasant appearance, Dark Olive Metallic not only exudes stability and a down-to-earth quality, but it also makes a true statement. Dark Olive Metallic was introduced with the 911 Turbo S (996) in the late 1990s and was later available on both the 911 (997) and the 718 Boxster and Cayman (987) model series. Depending on how light and shadow hit the surface of the vehicle, Dark Olive Metallic can shimmer from black to gold-green. Due to large metallic flakes in the paint, this can create a ‘flop effect’ that leaves a lasting impression.

name: Dark Sapphire Metallic
notes: Precious rarity: Darksapphiremetallic has a deep blue aura and is reminiscent of the precious gemstone sapphire. The color radiates a cool, calming effect and is a popular choice as an accent color for formal occasions – evening dresses or suits are often made in Darksapphire due to the shimmer effect. Darksapphiremetallic was implemented at the request of a customer as part of the Paint to Sample Plus process from Porsche and is part of the Paint to Sample offering. Darksapphiremetallic is a rich shade of blue that can be described as both dark and intense. The color is striking and elegant, associating Darksapphiremetallic with a certain level of luxury. The color spectrum lies somewhere between Nightbluemetallic and Carbonblackmetallic.

name: Dark Sea Blue
notes: Contemporary: Darkseablue is a dark, non-metallic blue that is reminiscent of the color of the ocean at great depths. The saturated Darkseablue radiates a cool, calming effect and is associated with serenity and stability. This attractive shade of blue was first available on the 911 Carrera in the late 1980s, followed by the 924S, 928 and 944 models. The color was also offered on the Cayenne SUV model in the 2003 model year. Darkseablue is an elegant dark blue that appears blue during the day and almost black at night or in poor lighting conditions. A special shade that loves to shine in the sun and embodies a sophisticated and elegant look.

name: Dark Teal (Lago) Metallic
notes: Deep green beauty: Dark Teal Metallic is a dark, rich shade of metallic blue-green that radiates its own attractiveness and elegance thanks to the glossy effect. “Lago” stands for lake in Italian and is reminiscent of the rich colors of mountain lakes. Dark Teal Metallic can be described as a mixture of a dark green and a deep blue, with a higher proportion of blue achieving the specific dark shimmer.

name: Deep Blue Metallic
notes: Deep Blue Metallic is a dark, saturated blue finished with a fine metallic flake that adds depth and dimension. In low light it can read nearly navy, while brighter conditions reveal a richer blue tone with subtle metallic highlights.

name: Delphi Green Metallic
notes: Delphi Green, like most 914 colors was part of Volkswagen's palette. It's a bright golden lime green that looks striking on Porsche's first mid-engine roadster. A similar color called Peridot Metallic has been offered more recently.

name: Desert Beige Metallic
notes: An elegant blend of sand, bronze, and champagne — the kind of color that makes a Porsche look sculpted from sunlight. Desert Beige Metallic has that unmistakable late-’70s to early-’80s vibe: luxurious but restrained, with a fine metallic flake that adds depth without flash. It catches light beautifully — shifting from soft gold in the sun to a cooler taupe in shade. The effect is timeless sophistication, the kind of tone that makes a classic 911 or 928 look both vintage and modern at once. It’s the color for someone who appreciates quiet power — understated, refined, and perfectly period-correct.

name: Diamond Black Metallic
notes: Porsche's Diamond Black Metallic is a deep black paint with a metallic shimmer that creates a diamond-like sparkle, achieved by metallic and mother-of-pearl pigments reflecting light in various directions. This gives the color a special depth and dimensionality, making it appear to have glittering, diamond-like sparkles that are more pronounced than in a standard black paint. The fundamental color is a rich, pure black. Fine metallic or mother-of-pearl pigments are suspended in the paint, giving it a distinctive shimmer and shine. A 2023 Paint-to- Sample (PTS) option which can indicate its rarity compared to standard colors.

name: Diamond Blue Metallic
notes: Diamond Blue Metallic is a light metallic shade that often reads as silver, with subtle blue and occasional lilac undertones emerging depending on the light. The color offers a softer, more nuanced alternative to traditional silvers, giving it a distinctive presence without appearing overtly blue.

name: Diamond Silver Metallic
notes: Diamond Silver was the silver choice for the entire 924 run in North America. Like many 924 colors, it was Audi-sourced.

name: Dolomite Grey Metallic
notes: Dolomite Grey Metallic is a fairly straightforward shade of gunmetal grey. Since it was a 924 color, it came from the Audi palette, and while it was a fairly common color on Audi 5000s and 4000s, and GT Coupes, it wasn't particularly common on the 924 where it was only offered in 1978 as part of the Special Edition package that consisted of Dolomite Grey paint, with black and white pinstripes and a special checkered Pasha cloth interior.

name: Dolomite Silver Metallic
notes: Porsche Dolomite Silver Metallic is yet another variation on the traditional German national racing color of silver. A little greyer than Arctic Silver, it complements the 718’s lines perfectly. It’s named for the Dolomite range of the Alps in Northern Italy.

name: Dolphin Gray
notes: Dolphin Grey is one of the lightest greys in the Porsche palette. It's almost a very cool white with a touch of blue/grey.

name: Dolphin Grey
notes: Dolphin Grey is a light pastel grey that is generally similar to Heron Grey, the earlier color that it replaced.

name: Dove Blue Metallic
notes: Dove Blue Metallic is a medium blue metallic with a softened, lightened appearance compared to deeper Porsche blues. Offered briefly during the transaxle era, it is understated and easily mistaken for more common shades, despite its distinct finish.

name: Eberlegreen
notes: Personal and individual: Eberlegreen is a rich shade of green that was originally developed at the request of a customer as part of Porsche's Paint to Sample Plus process and has been part of the Paint to Sample program portfolio since 2024. This means that Eberlegreen can also be ordered by other customers. The origin of the name Eberlegreen goes back to the surname of the customer, who was able to realise a dream by developing his very own personal paint color. Eberlegreen can be described as medium green. This striking color is similar to the paint color Essmanngreen, but is bluer and more muted in its expression.

name: Eisblau (Ice Blue)
notes: Porsche Eisblau (Ice Blue) is a light silver-blue color first offered in the early 1960s. Known for its subtle blue tint and clean metallic appearance, Eisblau presents as silver in low light and reveals more blue tones in brighter conditions. It remains a timeless and elegant choice among classic Porsche colors.

name: Emerald Green Metallic
notes: Strong presence: Emerald Green Metallic is a rich shade of metallic green that derives its name from the gemstone of the same name. Emerald Green Metallic is a radiant shade with subtle bluish undertones that exudes quality and has a strong presence. The color is often associated with nature, growth and renewal.

name: Espresso Brown Metallic
notes: Espresso Brown is a color that stands out next to any other, which you wouldn't think for a brown right? It is so warm and smooth but looks so classy at the same time. It truly is a tasty color.

name: Estoril Blue Metallic
notes: Estoril Blue Metallic is a rare Porsche color named after Estoril, Portugal, a location long associated with motorsport and European racing culture. Offered only briefly on the 987 and 997 platforms, it features a bright blue enhanced by subtle metallic flake. Though often mistaken for BMW’s Estoril Blue, the Porsche color is entirely distinct and unrelated, with visual roots closer to earlier Porsche shades like Gemini Blue.

name: Fashion Gray
notes: Fashion Grey was one of the earliest Porsche greys, and it has a habit of reappearing from time-to-time, first on an occasional 356B or C as a special order and more recently as a standard color across the Porsche line.

name: Fayence Yellow
notes: Visually, Fayence Yellow is quite similar to Speed Yellow, but if you see it on a Carrera GT, it's Fayence, named for a picturesque village in France.

name: Fern Green
notes: Fern Green is a non-metallic shade of blue/grey/green. It's a near mythical color, one model year only and rarely ordered.

name: Ferrari Yellow
notes: As the name implies, this particular yellow was inspired by the iconic Ferrari shade of Giallo Fly (Fly Yellow).

name: Fish Silver Grey
notes: Fish Silver Grey is a beautiful version of grey that you would not have otherwise thought of, but the paint looks like no other. In the dark it is a aggressive slick grey, and in the light it reflects colors and almost turns a little blue.

name: Fish Silver Grey Metallic
notes: Lighter Green is a color offered in the updated Paint To Sample program for 2023.

name: Fjord Green
notes: Fascinating inlet: Inspired by the impressive colors of the Norwegian coastal landscape, forests and waters, Fjordgreen conveys a strength that is both relaxing and balanced. The multifaceted green color is a paint finish of the Porsche 356 A, which was offered from the 1956 model year and bore the color code 6005. Fjordgreen is a complex and fascinating shade of green that embodies an extraordinary natural landscape: the colors of the deep water in a fjord, a narrow inlet surrounded by steep cliffs or mountains on the coasts of Scandinavia. The shade is almost blue, yet still green. It is a multi-layered color that embodies a calmness – reminiscent of the serenity and natural beauty of a fjord.

name: Flamingo Metallic
notes: Flamingo Metallic (which in spite of its name, doesn't have much of a pink cast to it) is another representative of the light platinum family that Porsche has consistently offered since the early 80s. It's a conservative choice for non-extroverts.

name: Forest Green Metallic
notes: What you see is what you get with the color name. It's one of the darker greens that Porsche has offered, and it is quite attractive.

name: Fountain Blue Metallic
notes: Rare, rarer, Fountainbluemetallic: This Porsche color is one of the rarest colors of the 911 generation (997) from 2011. The distinctive paint is somewhere between Azzurothetysmetallic and GTsilvermetallic and is named after the color of the water in the Palace of Fontainebleau in France, which is a combination of blue and green tones. At the request of a customer, Fountainbluemetallic was implemented as part of the Paint to Sample Plus process and was later incorporated into the Paint to Sample program. Fountainbluemetallic is a shade of blue that is often described as a light to medium blue with hints of grey or blue. The versatile color has a calming and soothing effect, creating a mood of serenity. The tone is able to make even unusual colors such as Nordicgoldmetallic or Malachitegreenmetallic appear almost ordinary.

name: Frozen Berry Metallic
notes: Electrifyingly beautiful: In keeping with the model expansion in 2021, the Porsche Taycan has also received its own color: Frozen Berry Metallic. The unmistakable rose color initially premiered as a series color and has been available in the Paint to Sample program since 2024. Frozen Berry Metallic is a pastel pink with a hint of blue undertones that looks delicate and very soft and is reminiscent of the historic Cassis Red Metallic from the 1980s.

name: Frozen Blue Metallic
notes: Frozen Blue Metallic is a modern Porsche blue introduced with the Taycan, blending a light, icy blue base with a fine metallic finish. The color has a cool, contemporary character that can evoke classic motorsport shades like Gulf Blue at a glance, though its metallic flake gives it a more modern, dimensional appearance. Compared to traditional light metallic blues such as Horizon Blue, Frozen Blue Metallic appears cleaner, brighter, and more saturated, especially in natural light.

name: Gabon Grey
notes: Gabon Grey is a very uncommon color from Porsche, and you will be one of the few if you get to lay your eyes upon it. Its a lighter grey with an almost beige/gold tint to it that sits perfectly underneath the sun.

name: Gambia Red
notes: A blander, darker red that almost resembles a maroon like color.

name: Garnet Red Metallic
notes: Garnet Red and Ruby Red Metallic are tough to distinguish. Like Ruby Red Metallic, this jewel-tone compliments just about any Porsche. Garnet is of course named for the semi-precious stone of the same name.

name: Gazelle Metallic
notes: A rare, sun-warmed bronze that sits somewhere between champagne and copper. Gazelle Metallic isn’t showy — it’s refined, understated luxury with a vintage twist. Under light, it shimmers with depth, shifting from soft gold to muted bronze. It’s a color that whispers class rather than shouting for attention — perfect on a car built for long drives and quiet confidence, not posing at the curb.

name: Gemini Blue Metallic
notes: Gemini Blue Metallic is a vivid metallic shade of electric blue that delivers strong color presence without feeling overpowering. The finish combines a bright, saturated blue with fine metallic flake, giving the color depth and energy in changing light. Gemini Blue looks equally at home on both short-hood and long-hood 911s, highlighting Porsche’s classic proportions while maintaining a distinctly period-correct character.

name: Gemini Grey Metallic
notes: Gemini Grey Metallic has a beautiful base tone of grey with a brighter almost gold shade under the light. It is truly one to see if you can.

name: Gentian Blue Metallic
notes: Captivating power: Gentianbluemetallic is a deep shade of Blue inspired by colorful gentian flowers. The bright Blue-Violet plant is native to the Alps and resembles the historic 356 model colors of Royal Blue and Cobalt Blue. Gentianbluemetallic is one of the strongest shades of Dark Blue that Porsche offers in its color program. Gentianbluemetallic is an intense Blue with Violet undertones. The striking color attracts attention and makes a clear statement as a captivating splash of color. The tone has a bright color intensity and looks exceptional in almost any lighting. The highly saturated color represents style, beauty and emphasizes the curves of every Porsche model.

name: Geyser Grey Metallic
notes: Geyser Grey Metallic is a color offered in the updated Paint To Sample program for 2023.

name: Glacier Blue (Dark)
notes: Glacier Blue (Dark) refers to the deeper early-1970s version of Glacier Blue, a color name Porsche has reused across multiple eras. This darker shade presents as a rich pastel blue with strong saturation, often compared to a lighter and softer interpretation of Mexico Blue. In contrast to the much paler Glacier Blue offered later on short-hood 911s and the 928, the early dark variant carries more visual weight and intensity, giving classic 911s a bold yet period-appropriate appearance.

name: Glacier Blue (Light)
notes: Glacier Blue (Light) is the later and much paler interpretation of the Glacier Blue name used by Porsche, distinct from the darker early-1970s version. This shade is an extremely light pastel blue that can appear nearly white in certain lighting, with only a soft hint of blue beneath the surface. It is sometimes mistaken for Gulf Blue, though Glacier Blue (Light) is noticeably lighter and more subdued, giving classic 911s a clean, airy appearance.

name: Glacier Metallic
notes: Glacier Metallic is a soft, light blue blended with subtle grey and white tones, creating a cool and restrained metallic finish. The color has an almost silvery appearance in low light, while gentle blue hues emerge more clearly in sunlight. Its understated character emphasizes body lines and surfaces rather than overpowering them, giving classic Porsche shapes a clean, technical look.

name: Glacier White (Modern)
notes: Cool, sharp, and subtly refined — Glacier White is Porsche’s interpretation of precision in paint. Unlike the warmer tones of Alpine or Grand Prix White, Glacier White carries a faint bluish undertone that gives it a crisp, icy edge. It’s the kind of white that feels engineered, not styled — modern, technical, and perfectly suited to Porsche’s performance DNA. In bright light, it shines with a pure, crystalline brightness — bright but not sterile. In shade, that subtle blue-grey depth appears, giving the surface a hint of cool metallic presence even though it’s a solid tone. The result is a clean, contemporary finish that makes body lines look sharper and proportions tighter.

name: Glacier White (Vintage)
notes: Glacier White is a clean, high-contrast hue with a faintly greenish undertone that gives it a subtle, icy edge. It’s not a warm or creamy white; it’s the color of precision, of aluminum and frost, it can look both sharp and modern as well as soft and nostalgic. In bright sunlight, it gleams with a cold brilliance — bright but never chalky. In softer or indirect light, that slight blue-gray cast gives it depth and a sense of cool restraint. There’s no pearl or metallic shimmer here — just a pure, technical white that feels cut from the same philosophy as Porsche’s design language: clean lines, purposeful surfaces, zero excess.

name: Glacier White Metallic
notes: Glacier White Metallic takes the purity of a bright white and infuses it with a fine metallic shimmer that adds depth without flash. It carries a subtle blue-silver undertone that gives it that “frozen” look — crisp and technical, yet undeniably elegant. In direct sunlight, it sparkles with an icy brilliance, highlighting every curve and crease like sunlight on fresh snow. Under softer light, it transitions to a silvery-white glow, calm and composed, with the metallic particles giving the surface a refined, liquid depth.

name: Gold Metallic
notes: Porsche Gold Metallic is a warm, yellow gold metallic that can best be described as roughly the color of a rich Pilsner beer. Given the German affinity for good beer, we'd hardly be surprised if that's what actually inspired this attractive, not often seen shade.

name: Golden Green
notes: Golden Green is one of the more literally descriptive Porsche color names. The color is exactly what it purports to be. Not a particularly popular color when new, it's kind of the flavor of the month at the moment among early 911 and 912 collectors.

name: Golden Yellow Metallic
notes: Golden Yellow Metallic is the color of the 911 Turbo S Exclusive Series. It was also the color chosen by Porsche for its "Project Gold" a 2018 build of a classic air-cooled 993 Turbo that sold for over $3 million at an auction at the Porsche Experience Center in Atlanta.

name: Golf Blue
notes: Half a century of history: Golfblue is a true classic amongst Porsche colors. The historic Porsche special color is also a historic Volkswagen special color and has been available for the 911 and the 912 (F model) since the mid-1960s. Golfblue is a non-metallic medium blue, but darker than the later well-known color Gulfblue, which are not to be confused. It is believed that the name and the color itself are both derived from the warm ocean current of the Gulf of Mexico. When looking at the Porsche 911 2.0 Coupé (original 911) from 1966 in Golfblue, your thoughts inevitably turn to the dimensions and vastness of the sea.

name: Gomera Metallic
notes: Wild and natural: the distinctive Gomera Metallic tone embodies the unique blend of the lush forests of the Spanish island with the Deep Blue of the surrounding Atlantic Ocean. Paired with its special glossy effect, Gomera Metallic is a color to immerse yourself in, reminiscent of the backdrop of natural landscapes, volcanic sand beaches and spectacular coastlines. The Audi color is now also used in the Porsche Paint to Sample program, adding a distinctive flair to vehicles with its vibrant colour brilliance. Gomera Metallic creates a naturally radiant atmosphere that has a silky shimmer thanks to the metallic effect. The Green tone, with its striking Blue undertone, delivers a uniquely invigorating experience.

name: Goodwood Green Metallic
notes: A legendary green: since 1993, the Goodwood Festival of Speed has been the largest automotive garden party in the world, set against the spectacular backdrop of Goodwood House in West Sussex. Every year, in front of over 180,000 spectators more than 600 vehicles are driven on the famous hill climb by equally legendary personalities from the world of automotive. Goodwood Green Metallic is used by Audi and Bentley and embodies this motorsport spirit – it is more than a homage to automotive individuality. Goodwood Green Metallic is a dark green color that looks elegant and, by its name alone, is associated with high-performance vehicles. The rich, deep green has a slightly bluish to greyish tint, making it appear slightly darker than many other shades of green. Porsche Racing Green Metallic is closest to the classic Goodwood Green Metallic, although light shades and different undertones appear here.

name: Grand Prix White
notes: Grand Prix White is the definitive performance white of the marque’s golden era. Introduced in the 1970s and forever tied to motorsport legends, it’s a clean, bright white with a subtle warmth that keeps it from feeling sterile. The result is a crisp, confident hue that captures the essence of Porsche’s racing DNA. In full sun, it pops — brilliant and high-contrast, the kind of white that makes every curve and crease read like sculpture. Under softer light, it settles into a pure, smooth tone with just a hint of creaminess, giving the car presence without flash. There’s no metallic, no pearl — just honest, high-gloss enamel that says “race car” even when it’s standing still.

name: Granite Green Metallic
notes: Granite Green is a smokey grey with a touch of green in it. It's an attractive, not often seen shade that just couldn't break the hold that reds, silvers, blacks and whites had on Porsche customers in the late 1980s.
